articles.title: GPT-5.4 mini and GPT-5.4 nano, which can describe 76,000 photos for $52
articles.summary: OpenAI has released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with significant cost efficiency improvements, enabling processing of thousands of images at much lower costs than previous generations.

articles.title: Bringing the power of Personal Intelligence to more people
articles.summary: Google is expanding Personal Intelligence across AI Mode in Search, the Gemini app and Gemini in Chrome. This feature allows AI to access user's personal Google ecosystem data for more tailored responses.

articles.title: Our latest investment in open source security for the AI era
articles.summary: Google is making new investments, building new tools and developing code security to improve open source security. The initiative focuses on AI-powered security solutions for the open source ecosystem.

articles.title: DOD says Anthropic's 'red lines' make it an 'unacceptable risk to national security'
articles.summary: The Defense Department has labeled Anthropic a supply chain risk due to concerns that the company might disable its technology during warfighting operations. This represents a significant escalation in tensions between AI safety advocates and military applications.

articles.title: Mistral bets on 'build-your-own AI' as it takes on OpenAI, Anthropic in the enterprise
articles.summary: Mistral Forge allows enterprises to train custom AI models from scratch on their own data, challenging rivals that rely on fine-tuning approaches. This represents a new enterprise strategy focused on fully customizable AI solutions.

articles.title: Investing in the people shaping open source and securing the future together
articles.summary: GitHub is investing in open source security by funding maintainers, partnering with Alpha-Omega, and expanding access to security tools. The initiative aims to reduce burden on maintainers while strengthening software supply chains.

articles.title: A Survey of Frontiers in LLM Reasoning: Inference Scaling, Learning to Reason, and Agentic Systems
articles.summary: Comprehensive survey categorizing LLM reasoning methods across inference scaling, learning to reason, and agentic systems. The paper provides a systematic framework for understanding different approaches to enhancing reasoning capabilities in large language models.

articles.title: Neural-Symbolic Logic Query Answering in Non-Euclidean Space
articles.summary: HYQNET addresses limitations in neural-symbolic models for complex logical queries on knowledge graphs. The approach better captures hierarchical query structures compared to existing methods.

articles.title: NextMem: Towards Latent Factual Memory for LLM-based Agents
articles.summary: Introduces a latent factual memory framework for LLM agents to preserve past observations without the limitations of textual or parametric approaches. Addresses challenges of catastrophic forgetting and high computational costs in agent memory systems.

articles.title: The Comprehension-Gated Agent Economy: A Robustness-First Architecture for AI Economic Agency
articles.summary: Proposes gating AI agents' economic permissions based on verified comprehension rather than capability benchmarks. Introduces formal architecture where economic agency is bounded by understanding rather than raw performance metrics.

articles.title: MiroThinker-1.7 & H1: Towards Heavy-Duty Research Agents via Verification
articles.summary: Presents research agents designed for complex long-horizon reasoning tasks with improved reliability through structured planning and verification. MiroThinker-H1 extends the base model with heavy-duty reasoning capabilities for multi-step problem solving.

articles.title: Nvidia has lost the plot with gamers
articles.summary: Nvidia's DLSS 5 technology that 'upgrades' video game character faces has faced significant backlash from gamers. The technology is seen as unnecessary modification of beloved game characters rather than genuine improvement.

articles.title: DLSS 5: Has Nvidia's AI graphics technology gone too far?
articles.summary: Nvidia's DLSS 5 introduces real-time neural rendering that can change game lighting and materials, but has received negative reception from gamers. Critics argue the technology 'yassifies' game characters and moves away from original artistic intent.

articles.title: The Pentagon is developing alternatives to Anthropic, report says
articles.summary: Following tensions with Anthropic, the Pentagon is reportedly developing alternative AI solutions for military applications. This suggests a permanent rift between the AI safety-focused company and defense applications.

articles.title: OpenAI expands government footprint with AWS deal, report says
articles.summary: OpenAI has reportedly partnered with AWS to sell AI systems to the U.S. government for both classified and unclassified work. This marks an expansion beyond the company's existing Pentagon deal.

articles.title: World launches tool to verify humans behind AI shopping agents
articles.summary: Sam Altman's World is expanding its verification services to support AI agent commerce by ensuring human identity behind shopping agents. The tool aims to prevent autonomous agent swarms from overwhelming online systems.

articles.title: Quoting Ken Jin
articles.summary: Community discussion or insight shared by developer Ken Jin, though specific details are not provided in the source snippet.

articles.title: Quoting Tim Schilling
articles.summary: Community perspective or technical insight from Tim Schilling, though specific content details are not available in the provided snippet.
